scrutinyOverride.rationale: Reviewer flagged two issues as blocking: (1) CLI onboard saves locally without relay registration, and (2) handle whitespace normalization gap. After independent assessment: (1) The relay endpoint POST /accounts/register EXISTS and correctly enforces global uniqueness — the CLI just doesn't call it yet, with an explicit phase-deferral comment. The building blocks are tested and functional. (2) The whitespace edge case requires intentional leading/trailing spaces in CLI args, and the HANDLE_PATTERN regex prevents internal spaces. Both are real but non-blocking gaps that can be addressed in future hardening.

nonBlockingIssues.description: CLI `mors onboard` persists profile locally without calling relay POST /accounts/register, so global duplicate-handle rejection is not enforced end-to-end in the CLI flow. The relay endpoint exists and works correctly — the CLI just needs to be wired to call it.

Handle validation uses trimmed value for format checks but registerAccount normalizes with untrimmed handle.toLowerCase(), allowing theoretical whitespace bypass of uniqueness. Fix: normalize with handle.trim().toLowerCase() in registerAccount.
CLI persistence test uses conditional assertion inside if(existsSync(profilePath)) which can pass even when profile persistence fails.
/events SSE path returns before auto-registration logic, so SSE-only authenticated devices are not registered. Device auto-registration only triggers on non-SSE authenticated requests.

Dedicated multi-device tests don't explicitly assert VAL-CROSS-006 secure-message continuity (key exchange + decrypt) for same-account multi-device flows. Coverage is indirect via cross/golden-path tests.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update mors-global-worker skill scope to include onboarding-handle-profile and autonomy-permissions milestones in the 'When to Use' section, since the orchestrator has been assigning features from these milestones to this skill.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both native-onboard-handle-profile-wizard and native-multi-device-bootstrap-continuity features use skillName mors-global-worker with milestone onboarding-handle-profile, but the skill's scope docs only list relay-foundation, global-async-messaging, realtime-watch, and developer-launch-path.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that workers should report followedProcedure=false when manual interactive checks are skipped, even if automated verification is thorough. Both workers in this milestone reported followedProcedure=true without interactiveChecks sections.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both feature handoffs (native-onboard-handle-profile-wizard and native-multi-device-bootstrap-continuity) have followedProcedure=true but no interactiveChecks, despite the skill requiring manual CLI/API/SSE sanity checks or explicit rationale for skipping.

rejectedObservations.observation: Mission convention guidance and test-code practice diverge on shell invocation patterns for dynamic args (test uses interpolated execSync).
rejectedObservations.reason: Test-only code with controlled inputs is low risk. The AGENTS.md convention about argv-based subprocess calls targets production code paths, not test harness helpers with static test-controlled values. Not actionable.
